setup.step1Desc: Choose a strong passphrase to encrypt your data. You'll enter this each time you open ThreatCaddy.
setup.step2Desc: Save this recovery key in a secure location. If you forget your passphrase, this is the only way to unlock your data.
setup.savedRecoveryKey: I've saved this recovery key in a secure location
setup.doNotCloseTab: Encrypting your existing data. Do not close this tab.
setup.passphraseMinLength: Passphrase must be at least 8 characters.
settings.httpsRequired: Encryption requires HTTPS or localhost. It is unavailable over plain HTTP.
settings.sessionEveryLoad: You'll enter your passphrase every time you open ThreatCaddy.
settings.sessionTabClose: Your session stays unlocked until you close the tab.
settings.sessionDuration_desc: Your session stays unlocked for {{duration}} after entering your passphrase.
settings.encryptionDesc: Encrypt your data at rest using AES-256-GCM. You'll set a passphrase to unlock your data (session duration is configurable after setup).
settings.disableDesc: Enter your passphrase to decrypt all data and disable encryption.
settings.newPassMinLength: New passphrase must be at least 8 characters.
passphrase.enterRecoveryKey: Enter your 24-word recovery key to unlock your encrypted data.
passphrase.enterPassphrase: Enter your passphrase to unlock your encrypted data.
passphrase.startFreshWarning: This will permanently delete all your encrypted data (notes, tasks, timelines, etc.). This cannot be undone.
